# Publication boundary and companion surfaces

This repo now has three distinct publication roles.

## 1. Interactive dashboard

The Next.js dashboard is the current-results explorer. It is the right place
for:

- reform-level comparison
- current static and dynamic outputs
- special-case baseline views
- direct access to downloadable current data products

It is not the right place to carry the full narrative burden of methods,
bibliography, and citation framing.

## 2. Citable manuscript

This Quarto paper is the stable narrative layer intended for:

- SSRN submission
- CRFB citation and external reference
- methods exposition
- benchmark framing
- appendices and bibliography

The manuscript should preserve the source trail and substantive chapter
coverage of the first report while updating scope to the current fourteen-option
package.

## 3. Operational documentation

Operational documentation answers a different question: not “what does the
analysis conclude?” but “what exactly did we run, validate, and decide?”

Those materials include:

- current methodology and pipeline notes
- reproducibility details
- audit findings and sentinel evidence
- delivery rules governing dashboard versus spreadsheet outputs

## Citation architecture

The clean long-run setup is:

- cite the paper for narrative interpretation and methods
- use the dashboard to explore current scenario outputs
- use the operational docs to audit provenance and rerun decisions

That split keeps the public-facing interpretation surface stable and citable
without forcing the dashboard to double as the full methodological record.

## Release discipline

That separation only works if each surface is honest about result status.

- If the standard static panel is clean but a special-case rerun is still in
  flight, the paper should say so directly rather than implying a fully settled
  fourteen-option package.
- The dashboard may continue to expose the latest assembled special-case values,
  but the manuscript should reserve “final” language for runs that have cleared
  the full validation ladder.
- Operational notes should carry the detailed rerun state, launch identifiers,
  and recovery details needed to reconcile those two publication surfaces.

For the current static package, that release discipline has been satisfied: the
standard panel was rebuilt on the hardened exact-only path, and the special-case
panel was recovered as a complete `2035-2100` artifact set before the delivery
bundle was rebuilt.

For the current dynamic package, the release surface is narrower but now
settled: the standard dynamic panel (`option1-12`) has been recovered to a
complete `2026-2100` artifact, while special-case dynamic scenarios are
intentionally excluded from the current public delivery. Those scenarios would
require a separate iterative balanced-fix solve after behavioral response, and
that extra compute path is not part of the release contract for this package.
